description_long: This is a large, L-shaped school building with a prominent terracotta-tiled gable roof and a white exterior. The structure features multiple rows of rectangular windows on each floor, providing ample natural light to the interior spaces. The main entrance is marked by a small, covered portico with steps leading up to double doors, suggesting a formal entry point. The building's design is functional, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation, typical of institutional architecture. The rear and side facades reveal additional access points and stairwells, as well as a partially recessed section that may serve as a secondary entrance or service area. The overall appearance is solid and utilitarian, suitable for an educational environment in an urban setting.
description: A large, L-shaped school building with a terracotta-tiled roof, white exterior, and multiple rows of rectangular windows. The main entrance features a covered portico and steps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a covered portico, steps, and symmetrical rows of windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long expanse of the building, tiled roof, and additional window rows; rear section visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view revealing the L-shape, recessed areas, and secondary access points.
description_view_3: Back corner view showing stairwells, side facade, and the continuation of the tiled roof.
